name: User Journeys
related_stage_3_constraints: async receipt and provider processing states

ux_decisions.decision: Center the MVP around a trip workspace with public page, private member workspace, and private event subspaces.
ux_decisions.rationale: Stage 3 defines distinct public, private trip, and private event trust boundaries.

ux_decisions.decision: Treat external contribution checkout as a clear off-platform handoff.
ux_decisions.rationale: Crewly must not imply it processes, holds, refunds, disputes, or pays out contribution funds in MVP.

journeys.name: Organizer creates and publishes a trip
journeys.start_state: Organizer has no structured trip hub.
steps: connect or paste external contribution provider target
journeys.success_state: Trip has a private workspace and a public page with an external-provider contribution CTA.
recovery_paths: publish without contribution CTA until provider target is configured

journeys.name: Public supporter reviews and contributes
journeys.start_state: Supporter opens a public trip page.
steps: review visible updates and contribution context
return to public trip page if available
journeys.success_state: Supporter understands they are contributing through an external provider and can return to Crewly for public updates.
recovery_paths: allow supporter to continue reading public updates
offer retry when provider link is available

journeys.name: Member logs expense and localizes receipt
journeys.start_state: Trip spending occurred and needs to be recorded.
steps: view translation and display currency values
manually correct source fields if needed
journeys.success_state: Expense and receipt are visible to authorized trip members with original values preserved.
recovery_paths: show original receipt without derived display values

journeys.name: Member creates private event and tracks event expenses
journeys.start_state: Subset of approved trip members needs private side activity tracking.
journeys.success_state: Event participants see event-specific balances separate from the trip ledger.
failure_states: selected participant is not approved trip member

journeys.name: Organizer publishes updates and public highlights
journeys.start_state: Trip has updates or media that may be shared with selected audiences.
journeys.success_state: Content appears only to the selected audience.

journeys.name: Admin reviews reports and audit events
journeys.start_state: A report, support request, or audit review need exists.
steps: inspect public content or scoped audit event
journeys.success_state: Report is resolved and audit trail is preserved.

risks_and_constraints.risk: Users may confuse external contribution status with Crewly-held funds.
risks_and_constraints.mitigation: Use off-platform handoff language and avoid balance/custody labels for provider references.

stage_5_handoff_notes: Implementation slices should map each journey to screens, APIs, permissions, and state tests.
